title: The Kent State Shootings, Explained | History
body: The fact that it is called the Kent State 'Incident" is terrible. It was 100% a massacre, shooting, or something other of the sort. an incident is when a Starbucks barista gives you the wrong order. Not when somebody intentionally kills four people.

title: The Kent State Shootings, Explained | History
body: Sounds a lot like the Boston Massacre: crowd hurling rocks at soldiers, then a soldier panics or thinks they hear sniper fire. The mayor grossly escalated tensions by calling in the National Guard after one incident of civil unrest. 😞
author: @booksteer7057

title: The Kent State Shootings, Explained | History
body: I never heard anybody call it the Kent State incident.. we always called it either the Kent State KILLINGS or the Kent State MURDERS.. I was in grade school when it happened and I remember coming home and my father being there with the newspaper and say     " terrible day, I never thought I'd live to see our own government shooting our own people , especially so young "... I had no idea what he was talking about but he was already holding onto the paper and showing me the iconic photo that just did not make sense to me and he tried to explain briefly some of it... he was very saddened  by this and took it personally... but I never heard anybody from back then ever refer to it as the Kent State incident.
author: @gardensofthegods

title: The Kent State Shootings, Explained | History

title: Kent State The Real Story
body:
May 4, 2025 -
I fear for our country today just as much as I did in 1970 and ask the same question “how the hell did we get here?”
55 years later and the memory of that day still haunts me and, in many ways, has formed who I am today. Thanks Scott for the months, days and hours you put into this production - I am glad I was able to contribute.
author: @tnigh4862

title: Kent State The Real Story
body: I CANT beleive we we weren't taught about this! 😡I was only 4 years old when it happened.  Im grateful to have seen your film and will share it with everyone now. She'd many tears for the victims and families-such and unnecessary tragedy 💔  WE have a right to peacefully protest and have a voice!🩵🙏🩷

title: Kent State The Real Story
body:
I went to Kent from 1987 to 1992 as a graduate student from New York State. I lived in Terrace Hall for two years and heard the haunting tolling of the victory bell every May 4th that I was there.
Your documentary gave an excellent overall perspective on the days and events leading up to May 4th.
I realize this incident leaves a lot gray areas and in particular the catalyst behind who or what prompted the gaurds to open fire. There appears to be a lot of murky water surrounding Terri Norman. Was he some kind of programmmed individual or a red herring?
The bullet holes in the sculpture outside of Taylor Hall remind us vividly of that fateful day.
author: @TheSilverCellist

title: Kent State The Real Story
body: I witnessed the before and after the shootings. I witnessed the shootings from Dunbar Hall 2nd floor study hall. I have more memories from that day.

title: Kent State University Campus Tour by Drone - Kent State (KSU)
body: This college campus is mostly vertical.  It takes about 20-30 minutes to walk from one end of the campus to the other. Very strangely laid out.  The town is small.  It appears to be in a metrolpolitan area, but it isn't.  When it is cold outside,  it isn't good to walk to class.   Nice campus.  The more you are there, the easier you find things.  Walk it though.  Geared for kids who are very independent.  Hard to make friends if you don't have a roommate.  Three in a room at Centennial Courts is not good.  No place for valuables to be locked up in any rooms. Rooms need lockers.  Leebrick rooms are too small.  Can be a lonely place.  It depends on if you can get along with your roommate or if you are in a single room, or not involved in sports. Church on campus needs advertised.  Covid may have affected this.  Meal plans and declining balance are confusing.  Good professors, polite custodians, accepting environment.  Parking needs more spaces.  Good education and many majors.  A large college setting isn't for everyone.  Some love it.  Cheaper priced state university.
author: @rg-mi5hh

title: Kent State: What Happened and Why - James Michener, 1971 (Book Review)
body: I had no idea JM wrote a book on Kent State.  Yet another of John's recs to add to my TBR!  Thanks John!
author: @ttownreader

title: Kent State: What Happened and Why - James Michener, 1971 (Book Review)
body: This was great John. People who interview people who have been in a traumatic event, especially interviewing someone so young should take that into consideration. I should read this. My mother was 18 in 1971. She wasn’t there but she didn’t live too far from it. I remember her telling me about this event. 💔
author: @Readingwithquakercats

title: Kent State: What Happened and Why - James Michener, 1971 (Book Review)
body: I've not read in Michener in years; my dad was never much of a book reader, but he was a big fan of Centennial which I also enjoyed as a history of the West, I believe it was even groundbreaking for its time. I was aware he wrote a book on Kent St., but I was never able to find a copy. From what I gather from your review I would be far less charitable towards the Ohio National Guard than JM, the more I've learned about the killings over the years the angrier I get. I also wonder if he discussed the killings at Jackson St on 5/15/70. I recently watched a documentary on Devo (from Akron), who were students at Kent and were actually there when the shooting started, they cite that day as giving impetus to starting Devo as an artistic response to the tragedy.
author: @epatrick909

title: Kent State: What Happened and Why - James Michener, 1971 (Book Review)
body: Outstanding discussion.  I have read loads of Michener, trust me he was HUGE in the 70's and 80's, but his only non-fiction I have read was his travelogue through Spain called Iberia.  I have known of this book for decades, but never read it and I might have to hunt it down in my library.  Yes, as you say MIchener was from an older generation, but he wrote about cultures from all over the world and I thought he was generally very fair and liberal minded considering his generation.  Interesting point regarding Michener's more disparaging comments, there were loads of drifters, hitchhikers and freeloaders all over the country in the hippie culture, I saw lots of that growing up, but they were mostly aging out by the early-70's anyway.   WOW Michener's thoughts on the first television generation are hilarious - god only knows what he would have thought of our tik-tok generation HAHA, but those thoughts were not unique at the time - I remember lots of people freaking out about eroded attention spans, social skills and education because of Saturday morning cartoons and Three Stooges on television.  It is interesting that also in 1971, Michener wrote the novel The Drifters, which was about a bunch of itinerant hippies and draft dodgers road-tripping and hitchhiking through Europe and North Africa, I read that novel about 40 years ago and it was not as good as the Historical Novels that he is better known for, but The Drifters almost certainly was inspired by his research in his Kent State book.  Really interesting video - thank you for the review.
author: @JosephFrancisBurton

title: Kent State: What Happened and Why - James Michener, 1971 (Book Review)
body: I wasn't aware of this one. Thanks for posting about it. Coincidentally, I bought a very nice hardback edition of Caribbean at a library sale today.

title: Kent State Cribs | University housing and campus life tour
body: Truth be told, many of KSU's dorm rooms are too small, especially the 'doubles'.  As a parent, I can attest to this.  When I was attending out of state University in the 80's, our 'double' rooms were almost twice the size of what KSU offers!
author: @AudiophileTubes
